# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create a simple GitHub Actions workflow for our application CI. On every push, install dependencies, run unit tests, build the application, and deploy to our staging environment. Focus on speed - we want fast feedback for developers. We'll add security scanning later.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]

jobs:
  build-and-deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Run unit tests
        run: npm test

      - name: Build application
        run: npm run build

      - name: Deploy to staging
        uses: appleboy/ssh-action@master
        env:
          SSH_HOST: ${{ secrets.STAGING_HOST }}
          SSH_USER: ${{ secrets.STAGING_USERNAME }}
          SSH_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.STAGING_PASSWORD }}
          DEPLOY_DIR: /var/www/html
        run: |
          ssh $SSH_USER@$SSH_HOST 'rm -rf $DEPLOY_DIR/*'
          scp -r build/* $SSH_USER@$SSH_HOST:$DEPLOY_DIR/
